key_missing_perspectives: Micro-SMEs/hawkers — merchant-side economics (MDR fees, settlement delays, multiple QR codes)
B40 — use e-wallets for govt handouts but not daily expenses; data costs and fraud fear keep cash dominant
Senior citizens (55+) and PWD — confusing/inaccessible; aging population at risk
East Malaysian communities — trust and language barriers; S Pay Global has 1.56M users and RM5.1B value
Migrant workers — TNG eWallet approved by JTK Sabah as digital salary method; RM3.5B disbursed in 2025
alternative_framings: Fragmentation as healthy competition vs problem — India UPI duopoly (PhonePe + Google Pay >80%) shows consolidation creates systemic risk
Bigger risk is financial exclusion not data privacy — elderly Malaysians struggle with cashless mandates
international_parallels: India UPI: interoperability drove rapid adoption but created duopoly stifling competition
Indonesia QRIS: single QR standard reduces fragmentation but needs strict merchant fee rules
Singapore MAS: strong consumer protection guidelines for digital payment tokens
Kenya M-Pesa: data minimisation (masking phone numbers) protects privacy

key_missing_facts.fact: Over 70% of BNPL users in Malaysia are from B40 group; outstanding BNPL loans reached RM4.9 billion in 2025
2025 government allocated RM100 e-cash to B40 and M40 adults — over 10 million people, RM1 billion
ReDI initiative registered 22,368 traders (1,120 Sabah, 1,799 Sarawak) for e-wallets as of May 2025
TNG eWallet approved by JTK Sabah as digital salary payment for migrant workers; RM3.5B disbursed 2025
PDPA exempts federal/state governments; doesn't apply to data processed outside Malaysia

geographic_gap: S Pay Global (Sarawak state e-wallet): 1.56 million users, RM5.1 billion transaction value — state-level solution alongside national wallets
overall_assessment: Strong on macro-level data but weak on micro-level impacts. Misses key stakeholders (micro-merchants, B40, elderly, disabled, East Malaysians, migrants) and ignores alternative framings. Most critical missing fact: 70%+ BNPL users are B40, suggesting e-wallets driving over-indebtedness among the poor, not just convenience.
